introduction: Data Definition Language (DDL) consists of SQL commands that define the database schema. It simply deals with descriptions of the database schema and is used to create and modify the structure of database objects in the database.

sections.title: CREATE Command
sections.content: Used to create the database or its objects (like table, index, function, views, store procedure and triggers).

sections.title: ALTER Command
sections.content: Used to alter the structure of the database. You can add, delete, or modify columns in an existing table.
sections.code: ALTER TABLE Students ADD Email varchar(100);

sections.title: DROP Command
sections.content: Used to delete objects from the database. A DROP statement removes a table entirely from the database.

sections.title: TRUNCATE Command
sections.content: Used to remove all records from a table, including all spaces allocated for the records are removed. The table structure remains.
